session.completion: clean verdict after local semantic and mutation checks

structured_output.summary: The patch accepts only the measured cmd.exe pair: one system-root conhost.exe by name/path and one registered daemon by name/path/command. It rejects every other raw-child shape, records console-host identity, and preserves exact recovery, teardown, and listener checks.
structured_output.guard_proof: Bash syntax, the 96-arm no-SSH self-test, and 77 analyzer tests passed. Changing only the production raw child requirement from two to one made the self-test fail; restoring exact reviewed bytes returned all checks green and left the worktree clean.

checks.classification_audit: exact normalized system conhost name/path plus exact daemon name/path/command; raw count exactly two
checks.teardown_audit: existing exact daemon recovery, launcher teardown, late-child refusal, and listener closure remain unchanged
checks.guard_mutation: production raw child count two to one failed the self-test; exact restoration returned green

policy_note: This is tactical advisory code review, not a formal openreview acceptance verdict.
